$schema: Echo seed dataset. Edit and re-run scripts/seed_full_demo.sh.

users.bio: Backend engineer. SQL nerd. Recovering microservices fan.
Game dev. Speedrunner. Mech keyboard hoarder.
Music producer by night, frontend dev by day.
Cinephile. Trying to watch every film in the Criterion Collection.
Security researcher. Will absolutely audit your auth flow.
Marathon runner, weekend tinkerer, dad jokes welcome.
Author of three sci-fi novels nobody asked for.

messages.text: anyone else moved over to sqlx 0.8 yet? the migration story finally feels solid
we did last sprint. the only gotcha was the new query macro hint format
I switched a side project, no regrets. their CI is nice now too
+1, also the fix for that prepared-statement caching bug landed in 0.8.4
tangent: anyone seen the new Tokio scheduler benchmarks?
the cooperative scheduling thing? yeah it's a real win for our hot paths
we saw a 12% latency drop on the websocket fanout after the upgrade
that matches what the post-mortem on their blog claimed
I'd love to see a flamegraph before/after if anyone has one handy
I'll throw one in here later today

replies.text: the new hint format threw us off too — tracking issue #2347 has the migration steps

messages.text: anyone up for ranked tonight around 9?
in. need to climb out of plat
I can do 2 games, then need to call it
I'll spectate, working on a deadline
did anyone finish the new soulslike yet? worth picking up?
boss design is top tier, world's a bit empty mid-game
the pvp invasions are killing me, in a good way
music's incredible, the boss themes alone justify the price
sold. picking it up tonight

replies.text: if you like methodical exploration, yes. don't go in expecting open-world fluff

groups.name: Music Corner
groups.description: Share tracks, discover artists, talk theory.

messages.text: fell down a rabbit hole on minimal techno from the early 90s last night
give me three names to start with
Robert Hood, Jeff Mills, Maurizio. start with Hood's Minimal Nation
Maurizio is so good. that whole basic channel catalog is pristine
anyone been to a Berghain residency? on my bucket list
haven't, but a friend went in 2019 and said the soundsystem alone is worth the trip
thanks for the recs. queueing them now
if you want a softer entry point, try Ricardo Villalobos' Easy Lee

replies.text: Minimal Nation is on right now. this is going on every commute playlist

messages.text: watch party Friday — voting between The Conversation and Memories of Murder
Memories of Murder. the tonal shifts are unreal
Conversation for me, Coppola era is unbeatable
split vote. flip a coin?
calling it: Memories of Murder Friday, Conversation the week after. 8pm in the lounge
I'll bring the wine
I'll do snacks. anyone gluten free?

replies.text: yes, eve is. picking up some rice crackers and dip

messages.text: good morning. who's already on their second coffee
third, but who's counting
I switched to matcha last week and it's been a journey
matcha-pilled. respect
fyi, the bookstore on 9th has 30% off all classics this weekend
ooh, going on Saturday if anyone wants to make a thing of it
in. let's get lunch after?
send me the address, will try to swing by
anyone seen the bug where the app shows time as 'in 3 minutes' for messages just sent? haunting
yes, my client clock was off by 2 minutes. fixed when I synced ntp
amazing, thanks. classic clock skew

replies.text: any cookbook recs? trying to expand the rotation
the Diana Henry section is huge right now. Salt Sugar Smoke is my pick

groups.name: Book Worms
groups.description: Reading lists, reviews, and literary discussion.

messages.text: currently reading: Piranesi by Susanna Clarke. anyone else?
I read it last year, the prose is hypnotic
on my list. is it as weird as Jonathan Strange?
different weird. shorter, dreamier, more melancholic
what's next on the list? I want a recommendation
Stoner by John Williams. quiet but devastating
I've heard nothing but good things. ordering it now

replies.text: putting it next on the queue. love a quiet devastating book
